title: eriogonum nummulare var. ammophilum
intro: Eriogonum nummulare var. ammophilum, also known as Desert Buckwheat, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its clusters of small white flowers and distinctive seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Desert Buckwheat include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of various respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum nummulare var. ammophilum can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various ways.
